title: Jack and the Beanstalk
content:
Once upon a time there lived a poor widow and her son Jack. They had nothing but a cow.

One day, Jack's mother said, "We have no money and no food. You must take the cow to market and sell her."

On his way to market, Jack met a strange old man.

"I will give you five magic beans for your cow," said the man.

Jack thought the beans looked wonderful. He traded the cow for the beans.

When Jack came home, his mother was furious. She threw the beans out the window.

The next morning, Jack saw a giant beanstalk reaching up to the clouds!

Jack climbed up, up, up the beanstalk. At the top, he found a huge castle.

Inside lived a giant! "Fee-fi-fo-fum!" roared the giant.

Jack hid while the giant counted his gold coins and fell asleep.

Jack grabbed a bag of gold and climbed down the beanstalk.

"We're rich!" cried his mother. But the gold soon ran out.

Jack climbed the beanstalk again. This time he took a hen that laid golden eggs!

One more time, Jack climbed the beanstalk. He found a magic harp that could sing.

But this time the giant woke up! "Fee-fi-fo-fum! I smell a boy!"

Jack grabbed the harp and ran. The giant chased him down the beanstalk.

"Mother, bring the axe!" Jack shouted.

Jack chopped down the beanstalk, and the giant fell with a mighty crash.

With the hen and the harp, Jack and his mother lived happily ever after.

The End.

author: Traditional

attribution.author: Traditional (English Fairy Tale)
attribution.source: English Fairy Tales, collected by Joseph Jacobs (1890)
